file_name: Monsanto Company - SECOND A_R EXCLUSIVE AGENCY AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Agent shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Contractor shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agent" to "Contractor" introduces uncertainty. While the initial clause refers to 'The Agent,' subsequent sentences within the same section contradictorily use 'Contractor' without defining it, which is causing an in-text contradiction. It's unclear whether 'Contractor' refers to the same entity as 'Agent', thereby affecting liability for sub-agent actions and reporting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Contractor shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Agent" with "Contractor." The contradicted text then continues to refer to both "Agent" and "Contractor" as if they are distinct entities, leading to a contradiction. It's unclear if "Contractor" is meant to be a substitute for "Agent" or a separate entity altogether, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding responsibilities and obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall cooperate with Monsanto, and promptly take such actions as requested by Monsanto, with respect to any defective product including any "stop-sales" or recalls for Roundup Products.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall cooperate with Monsanto, and promptly take such actions as requested by Monsanto, with respect to any defective product including any "stop-sales" or recalls for Licensed Products.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Roundup Products" to "Licensed Products" creates a contradiction. The original definition of 'Roundup Products' is known within the contract, however, the term "Licensed Products" is now used without definition, raising questions about whether this broader term encompasses products beyond Roundup Products, which introduces uncertainty as the definition is unknown.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent shall cooperate with Monsanto, and promptly take such actions as requested by Monsanto, with respect to any defective product including any "stop-sales" or recalls for Licensed Products.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Roundup Products" to "Licensed Products" introduces ambiguity because "Licensed Products" is not defined in the contract, while "Roundup Products" likely has a defined meaning. This creates a contradiction because the scope of the Agent's cooperation regarding defective products is now unclear. The Contradicted Text, which uses the undefined "Licensed Products," now clashes with the implicit understanding or potential explicit definition of "Roundup Products" elsewhere, creating uncertainty about the agent's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Monsanto determines to consummate a Roundup Sale with a party other than the Agent, Monsanto shall deliver the Agent notice thereof and of the identity of such other party. Within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice, the Agent shall deliver written notice to Monsanto stating either that: (1) The Agent intends to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b), in which case such notice shall constitute a termination notice for purposes of this Agreement provided that the termination shall be effective at the end of the Third Program Year following the Program Year in which the Agent delivers its Notice of Termination pursuant to this provision; or (2) The Agent will not terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b) and agrees to continue the performance of its obligations under the Agreement unless and until the Agent receives a termination notice delivered in accordance with the terms of this Agreement by the successor to the Roundup Business.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Monsanto determines to consummate a Roundup Sale with a party other than the Agent, Monsanto shall deliver the Agent notice thereof and of the identity of such other party. Within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice, the Exclusive Agent shall deliver written notice to Monsanto stating either that: (1) The Exclusive Agent intends to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b), in which case such notice shall constitute a termination notice for purposes of this Agreement provided that the termination shall be effective at the end of the Third Program Year following the Program Year in which the Agent delivers its Notice of Termination pursuant to this provision; or (2) The Exclusive Agent will not terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b) and agrees to continue the performance of its obligations under the Agreement unless and until the Agent receives a termination notice delivered in accordance with the terms of this Agreement by the successor to the Roundup Business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agent' to 'Exclusive Agent' in specific sentences introduces a contradiction. The term "Exclusive Agent" has not been defined, and the perturbation occurs in this section. This can introduce an in-text contradiction as it creates confusion as to which "Agent" are the obligations intended to, leading to ambiguity in enforcement, this is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Monsanto determines to consummate a Roundup Sale with a party other than the Agent, Monsanto shall deliver the Agent notice thereof and of the identity of such other party. Within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice, the Exclusive Agent shall deliver written notice to Monsanto stating either that: (1) The Exclusive Agent intends to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b), in which case such notice shall constitute a termination notice for purposes of this Agreement provided that the termination shall be effective at the end of the Third Program Year following the Program Year in which the Agent delivers its Notice of Termination pursuant to this provision; or (2) The Exclusive Agent will not terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.5(b) and agrees to continue the performance of its obligations under the Agreement unless and until the Agent receives a termination notice delivered in accordance with the terms of this Agreement by the successor to the Roundup Business.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agent' to 'Exclusive Agent' in the changed text, while the contradicted text still refers to both 'Agent' and 'Exclusive Agent,' introduces ambiguity. Since 'Exclusive Agent' is not defined, it's unclear whether it refers to the same entity as 'Agent' or a different one. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction because the obligations and rights of 'Agent' are potentially different from those of 'Exclusive Agent,' leading to uncertainty and potentially conflicting obligations, aligning with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.
